commit.message:
Simplify: remove DB/auth, go fully stateless

- No database, no auth, no sessions — everything in memory
- Upload files → parse → aggregate → return JSON → render dashboard
- Single page: upload zone switches to dashboard on success
- Deleted: SQLite, Drizzle, iron-session, bcryptjs, all API routes except /upload
- Kept: parsers (CSV/PDF/Excel), aggregation logic, analytics, charts
- Vercel-deployable out of the box — no persistent storage needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: autonomous pipeline flow — user only at final merge

PASS paths no longer interrupt with AskUserQuestion. Each stage
auto-invokes the next:
- create-pr → direction-reviewer
- direction-reviewer PASS → code-reviewer
- code-reviewer PASS → acceptance

Only user touchpoints:
- Any FAIL (human decision required)
- Acceptance PASS: gh pr ready + notify user to merge

and open them in Obsidian as a vault. Covers editing tests in Obsidian
and pushing changes back to Testomat.io via CLI.

Also moves "Download Manual Tests as Files" into the Export tests
sidebar section as "Download as Markdown Files".

commit.message:
Multiplayer Craps game with real-time WebSocket rooms (#97)

## Summary

Full multiplayer craps game with real-time gameplay via Cloudflare
Durable Objects.

- **Real-time multiplayer** — WebSocket rooms with up to 4 players,
Clerk auth, room codes, and session takeover handling
- **Full craps engine** — Pass/Don't Pass, Come/Don't Come, Place,
Field, Hardways, and prop bets with correct casino payouts. Shared
engine between client and server with comprehensive test suite
- **Casino-quality UI** — Felt table with per-zone chip placement, 3D
dice with roll animations, roll history, hot streak tracker, payout
reference card, confetti on wins, and sound effects
- **Betting controls** — Chip selector, categorized bet panel,
lock/confirm flow, and real-time balance tracking with animated +/-
deltas
- **Chat system** — In-game chat with player colors and auto-scroll
- **Deep linking** — Direct room URLs (`/craps/ABCD`), preserves room
code through auth redirect
- **Consolidated worker** — Merged retro-worker into shared
realtime-worker serving both RetroRetro and Craps

### Notable fixes in this branch
- Auth redirect preserves room URL path instead of losing it on sign-in
- Bet controls no longer flash unlocked for one frame after dice roll
(race between state update and animation)
- DICE shooter tag uses theme orange instead of alarming red
- Dice always centered under table (roll total positioned absolutely)
- Layout reordered: table → betting controls → dice/roll button

## Test plan
- [ ] Create a room, copy invite link, join from another
browser/incognito
- [ ] Place various bet types, confirm bets, roll dice — verify payouts
- [ ] Test deep link: visit `/craps/XXXX` while signed out, sign in,
verify redirect back to room
- [ ] Verify bet controls stay locked during dice animation with no
flash
- [ ] Test chat messaging between players
- [ ] Test session takeover (open same room in two tabs)
- [ ] Verify RetroRetro still works after worker consolidation

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Quality: pmat quality-gate --fail-on-violation

commit.message:
Fix root domain: coming soon page at /, game at /games/...

Vercel always serves dist/index.html for / before rewrites fire, so
swapping files in a post-build script is the only reliable approach:
- post-build.mjs: renames dist/index.html → dist/game.html, copies
  public/root.html → dist/index.html
- vercel.json: game subpath rewrites now point to /game.html; no root
  rewrite needed since dist/index.html IS the coming soon page
- public/root.html: add link to the Dad Jokes game

Result: blackcafe.miami/ → Marly's Yard coming soon + game link
        blackcafe.miami/games/canyoukeepastraightface → Dad Jokes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add task analysis phase and update README

Major architectural improvement: Add task analysis phase before execution.

Changes:
- Add core/task-analyzer.js: Analyze user intent (search/browse) and extract
  keywords before navigation
- Add core/site-config.js: Hardcoded site configurations with search URL
  templates, browse URLs, and key selectors (including login modals)
- Update llm-agent.js: Integrate task analysis phase, construct initial URL
  based on intent (search uses ?q= params, browse uses homepage)
- Update core/prompt-builder.js: Inject task analysis context and site
  selectors into prompt
- Update README.md: Complete rewrite to reflect new 4-phase execution model
  (analyze → navigate → execute → summarize), remove outdated workflow docs

Benefits:
- Agent "thinks" before "acting" - analyzes task intent upfront
- Direct navigation to search results (skip manual search box input)
- Hardcoded selectors for accuracy (article lists, login modals)
- Clearer separation of concerns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add standalone Windows installer for NVIDIA and AMD Strix Halo

Standalone Windows installer package — zero changes to existing Linux code.
Supports NVIDIA (via Docker Desktop GPU passthrough) and AMD Strix Halo
(native llama-server.exe with Vulkan, everything else in Docker).

New files in dream-server/installers/windows/:
- install-windows.ps1: 6-step orchestrator (preflight, detection, features,
  setup, launch, verify) with --DryRun, --Tier, --Cloud, --All flags
- dream.ps1: CLI wrapper (status, start, stop, restart, logs, config, chat,
  update) with native llama-server lifecycle management for AMD
- lib/constants.ps1: version, paths, colors matching constants.sh
- lib/tier-map.ps1: byte-identical tier mappings from tier-map.sh
- lib/detection.ps1: GPU detection via nvidia-smi and WMI, Docker Desktop
  validation, Strix Halo unified memory heuristic
- lib/env-generator.ps1: .env generation with .NET crypto secrets, SearXNG
  and OpenClaw config generation, Windows ACL permissions
- lib/ui.ps1: CRT-themed output helpers matching the Linux installer voice
- docker-compose.windows-amd.yml: AMD overlay disabling Docker llama-server,
  routing containers to host via host.docker.internal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(checkin): corrige GPS no iPhone (iOS Safari)

Problemas corrigidos:
1. Auto-submit via QR removido — iOS Safari bloqueia getCurrentPosition
   silenciosamente quando não disparado por gesto do usuário (tap).
   Agora o usuário precisa tocar o botão, o que garante o gesto.

2. Erros de geolocalização diferenciados por código:
   - PERMISSION_DENIED (1): instrução específica para Ajustes > Safari > Localização
   - POSITION_UNAVAILABLE (2): instruir a ir ao ar livre
   - TIMEOUT (3): instruir a tentar novamente
   - Sem geolocation API: orientar a usar Safari/Chrome atualizado

3. Opções de GPS ajustadas para iPhone:
   - timeout: 10s → 20s (GPS demora mais em ambientes internos)
   - maximumAge: 30s adicionado (reutiliza posição em cache → sem warmup)

feat: autarkeia — agent export/import portability (#507)

## Summary

- **AgentFile schema** (`crates/mneme/src/portability.rs`) — portable
format with `serde(rename_all = "camelCase")` for TypeScript wire
compatibility
- **Export** (`crates/mneme/src/export.rs`) — workspace scanning with
binary detection, session/message/note extraction including distilled
messages and working_state/distillation_priming via raw SQL
- **Import** (`crates/mneme/src/import.rs`) — workspace restoration with
path traversal protection, session import with new IDs and
collision-safe session keys, note category validation
- **CLI** (`crates/aletheia/src/main.rs`) — `aletheia export <nous-id>`
and `aletheia import <file>` with `--dry-run`, `--force`, `--target-id`,
`--compact`, `--archived` flags

Architecture constraint respected: mneme accepts simple types (paths,
strings), CLI resolves Oikos/taxis and passes them down.

## Test plan

- [x] 4 portability schema tests (serde roundtrip, camelCase keys,
skip_serializing_if)
- [x] 7 export tests (binary detection, workspace scan, ignored dirs,
archived filtering, distilled messages, message limit)
- [x] 12 import tests (path traversal rejection, version validation,
workspace restore, force overwrite, session/message/note import,
roundtrip, target ID override, skip flags)
- [x] 2 TS-compat integration tests (fixture deserialization, roundtrip
preservation)
- [x] 3 CLI parse tests (export, export with output, import with all
flags)
- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-mneme -p aletheia` — 143 tests pass

---------

Co-authored-by: admin <admin@ardentleatherworks.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(dokimion): behavioral eval framework for pre-cutover validation (#504)

## Summary

- New `crates/eval/` (`aletheia-dokimion`) — behavioral evaluation
framework that runs scenarios against a live Aletheia instance via HTTP
- Leaf crate with zero workspace dependencies — treats the runtime as a
black box
- 14 built-in scenarios across 5 categories: health, auth, nous,
session, conversation
- CLI integration: `aletheia eval --url <url> --token <jwt> [--scenario
<filter>] [--json]`
- SSE stream parser for consuming pylon message responses
- 13 unit tests for SSE parsing, client construction, and report logic

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo clippy -p aletheia-dokimion -- -D warnings` passes
- [x] `cargo clippy -p aletheia -- -D warnings` passes  
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-dokimion` — 13 tests pass
- [ ] `aletheia eval` against a running instance validates
health/auth/session scenarios
- [ ] `aletheia eval --json` produces machine-readable output

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix generate-all timeout: skip already-generated workflows

Root cause: generate-all processed all 4 workflows sequentially (~48s each),
totaling 192s — past the 180s client-side AbortController timeout. The server
completed successfully but the browser killed the request, showing a failure.

- Skip workflows with aiGenerated=true in generate-all endpoint (0.4s vs 192s)
- Scale client timeout dynamically: 60s per ungenerated workflow, min 180s
- Only mark ungenerated workflows as "generating" in the UI spinner state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(relatorio-ibscbs): reclassify devoluções by goods flow direction

Devoluções de venda (out_refund) are goods ENTERING the company, so they
now appear under the "Entradas" filter instead of "Saídas". Conversely,
devoluções de compra (in_refund) are goods LEAVING, so they move to "Saídas".

commit.message:
fix(security): remove hardcoded API keys; null fake confidence scores

Critical security and trust fixes identified in codebase audit:

1. Hardcoded API keys removed (stock_service.py, options_service.py):
   - 4× Alpha Vantage key hardcoded in stock_service.py → os.getenv('ALPHAVANTAGE_API_KEY', '')
   - 1× Finnhub key hardcoded in options_service.py → os.getenv('FINNHUB_API_KEY', '')
   - Keys must now be set via environment variables (already in .env, gitignored)
   - Empty string fallback means API calls fail loudly rather than silently using a leaked key

2. Hash-based fake confidence score removed (main_server.py line 6850):
   - Was: 0.75 + (hash(symbol) % 20) / 100  ← deterministic fake, looks real to users
   - Now: None  ← explicit null; frontend must handle absence of confidence gracefully
   - This appeared in the fallback strategy path when OpenAI API key is unavailable

3. Fallback stock scoring clarified (ai_service.py):
   - ml_confidence: 0.5 → None (0.5 falsely implied "50% certain", actually meant "unknown")
   - ml_reasoning: now explicitly states FSS score only, AI unavailable
   - Added fallback_used: True flag so frontend can show appropriate unavailable state

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(hermeneus): dynamic credential provider with OAuth refresh (#506)

## Summary

- **`CredentialProvider` trait in koina** — zero-dependency interface
for per-request credential resolution. All crates can depend on it
without new dependency edges.
- **Four implementations in symbolon** — `EnvCredentialProvider` (env
var per-call), `FileCredentialProvider` (JSON file with mtime cache),
`RefreshingCredentialProvider` (background OAuth token refresh),
`CredentialChain` (ordered fallback).
- **`AnthropicProvider` accepts dynamic credentials** — `api_key:
SecretString` replaced with `Arc<dyn CredentialProvider>`. Headers
resolved per-attempt inside retry loop for mid-request rotation.
`from_config()` preserved via `StaticCredentialProvider` wrapper
(backward compat).
- **Resolution chain in aletheia binary** — file (with refresh if
refreshToken present) → env var. `aletheia credential status` and
`aletheia credential refresh` CLI commands.
- **Credential file format** — `{ "token": "...", "refreshToken": "...",
"expiresAt": <ms_epoch> }`. Compatible with existing TS/Python
credential scripts.

## Architecture

Trait in `koina` (not `symbolon` as prompt suggested) to preserve the
dependency graph — hermeneus already depends on koina, adding symbolon
would create a new edge. Implementations in `symbolon`, resolution in
`aletheia` binary (already imports everything).

## Files changed

| Crate | What |
|-------|------|
| `koina` | New `credential` module (trait + types) |
| `symbolon` | New `credential` module (4 implementations, 13 tests) |
| `hermeneus` | `AnthropicProvider` accepts `Arc<dyn
CredentialProvider>` |
| `aletheia` | Credential chain construction, CLI commands |
| `instance.example` | Credential file template + README |

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` — zero
warnings
- [x] `cargo test --workspace` — all pass (900+ tests)
- [x] 13 credential-specific tests: file roundtrip, chain ordering, env
fallback, file mtime detection
- [x] Existing hermeneus wiremock tests pass unchanged (backward compat)
- [ ] Manual: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=test cargo run -- credential status`
shows env source
- [ ] Manual: place credential file, verify file source detected

---------

Co-authored-by: admin <admin@ardentleatherworks.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: aletheia
repository.description: Distributed cognition system — infrastructure, tooling, and continuity

commit.message:
Add ATLAS — Adaptive Trading & Learning Architecture System

Self-upgrading cognitive knowledge engine with 6 learning modules:

- atlas/core.py: Central SQLite knowledge hub capturing 25-dimension
  context snapshot on every trade entry (technical + options + news +
  volume + regime + fundamental). Auto-builds pattern correlations.

- atlas/options_flow_memory.py: Historical PCR zone to outcome mapping.
  Tracks PCR trends, IV percentile zones, unusual OI spikes, max pain.

- atlas/news_impact_mapper.py: News event type to price impact causality.
  15 event categories (EARNINGS_BEAT/MISS, RBI_RATE_CUT/HIKE, etc.)

- atlas/regime_detector.py: Market regime detection (BULL/BEAR/SIDEWAYS/
  VOLATILE/RECOVERY) + time patterns (session, day, expiry week).

- atlas/volume_classifier.py: 9 volume spike types with win-rate tracking.
  BREAKOUT, ACCUMULATION, CLIMAX_BUY/SELL, DISTRIBUTION, INSIDER_SPIKE.

- atlas/causal_engine.py: Multi-dimensional WHY analysis on every trade.
  Primary cause, secondary causes, failure reason, 1-line lesson.

- atlas/self_upgrader.py: Nightly synthesis engine, rebuilds stats, generates
  GOLD/SILVER trading rules, calls Claude Haiku for narrative insights.

Integration: paper_trader logs full snapshot on entry, updates outcome on
close. 8 new API endpoints. Context refresh every 15min, full upgrade 21:00.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enforce security guardrails in scaffolded projects

Add four mandatory security policies enforced by hooks (hard-block, exit 2):
- Directory scope guard: locks file access to project root, blocks credential stores and .env files
- Secrets/PII guard: blocks AWS keys, GitHub PATs, tokens, SSNs, credit cards in output
- Environment protection guard: tiered deploy approval (prod/staging hard-block, dev advisory)
- Third-party content isolation: review-time checklist for prompt injection, unsafe deserialization

Also adds deny list to settings.json, updates security-reviewer agent with prompt injection
checklist, expands conventions with PII/third-party sections, and embeds guardrails in scaffold template.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
